devanagari: कुब्जायामनुग्रहः धनुषो भङ्गः मल्लशालासज्जीकरणं च । श्रीशुक उवाच । अथ व्रजन्राजपथेन माधवः । स्त्रियं गृहीताङ्गविलेपभाजनाम् । विलोक्य कुब...
english_devnagari: kubjāyāmanugrahaḥ dhanuṣo bhaṅgaḥ mallaśālāsajjīkaraṇaṃ ca śrīśuka uvāca atha vrajanrājapathena mādhavaḥ striyaṃ gṛhītāṅgavilepabhājanām vilokya kubjāṃ yuvatīṃ varānanāṃ papraccha yāntīṃ prahasan rasapradaḥ

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
While proceeding ahead on the royal road, Kṛṣṇa espied a deformed young woman of charming countenance carrying a vessel containing sandal-paste and other unguents (for smearing and beautifying the body). He asked laughingly just to humour her.

devanagari: का त्वं वरोर्वेतदु हानुलेपनं । कस्याङ्गने वा कथयस्व साधु नः । देह्यावयोरङ्गविलेपमुत्तमं । श्रेयः ततस्ते न चिराद् भविष्यति ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: kā tvaṃ varorvetadu hānulepanaṃ kasyāṅgane vā kathayasva sādhu naḥ dehyāvayoraṅgavilepamuttamaṃ śreyaḥ tataste na cirād bhaviṣyati

translation: “Who are you, Oh lady of shapely thighs? For whom is this vessel of unguents intended? Tell us the truth. Give us both some excellent unguents (out of these). (By this act of yours) you will soon be blessed with good luck.”

devanagari: सैरन्ध्रि उवाच । दास्यस्म्यहं सुन्दर कंससम्मता । त्रिवक्रनामा ह्यनुलेपकर्मणि । मद्‍भावितं भोजपतेरतिप्रियं । विना युवां कोऽन्यतमस्तदर्हत...
english_devnagari: sairandhri uvāca dāsyasmyahaṃ sundara kaṃsasammatā trivakranāmā hyanulepakarmaṇi mad‍bhāvitaṃ bhojapateratipriyaṃ vinā yuvāṃ ko

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The maidservant said:
“Oh beautiful youth! I am a maid-servant called Tri-Vakrā (Deformed at three places viz. at the neck, the breast and the waist), but I am esteemed by (His Majesty) Kaṃsa in (the technique of) preparation of unguents). For the pastes and ointments prepared by me are very much liked by the king of Bhojas.—who else other than you both deserves to be anointed with this?”

devanagari: रूपपेशलमाधुर्य हसितालापवीक्षितैः । धर्षितात्मा ददौ सान्द्रं उभयोरनुलेपनम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: rūpapeśalamādhurya hasitālāpavīkṣitaiḥ dharṣitātmā dadau sāndraṃ ubhayoranulepanam

translation: Her heart being thoroughly captivated by their beauty, comeliness, bewitching smiles, sweet words and loving glances, she besmeared them thickly with the unguent.

devanagari: ततस्तावङ्गरागेण स्ववर्णेतरशोभिना । सम्प्राप्तपरभागेन शुशुभातेऽनुरञ्जितौ ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: tatastāvaṅgarāgeṇa svavarṇetaraśobhinā samprāptaparabhāgena śuśubhāte
translation: Then with the upper part of their bodies painted with the unguent, the colour of which was different from that of the complexion of their persons, they both (Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma) appeared very much effulgent.

devanagari: प्रसन्नो भगवान् कुब्जां त्रिवक्रां रुचिराननाम् । ऋज्वीं कर्तुं मनश्चक्रे दर्शयन् दर्शने फलम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: prasanno bhagavān kubjāṃ trivakrāṃ rucirānanām ṛjvīṃ kartuṃ manaścakre darśayan darśane phalam

translation: The glorious Lord who was very much pleased with her, decided to straighten up perfectly the hump-backed woman deformed at the three different places, though possessed of a charming countenance, and thereby to show (to the world) the fruit of having a sight of him.

devanagari: पद्‍भ्यामाक्रम्य प्रपदे द्र्यङ्गुल्युत्तान पाणिना । प्रगृह्य चिबुकेऽध्यात्मं उदनीनमदच्युतः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: pad‍bhyāmākramya prapade dryaṅgulyuttāna pāṇinā pragṛhya cibuke

translation: Pressing the front part of her feet with his own feet, he held her chin with two fingers of his raised open palm and made her body perfectly straight.

devanagari: सा तदर्जुसमानाङ्गी बृहच्छ्रोणिपयोधरा । मुकुन्दस्पर्शनात् सद्यो बभूव प्रमदोत्तमा ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: sā tadarjusamānāṅgī bṛhacchroṇipayodharā mukundasparśanāt sadyo babhūva pramadottamā

translation: Instantly, at the very touch of Kṛṣṇa, she became the loveliest young damsel with all limbs straight and proportionate and shapely, with big buttocks and heavy breasts.

devanagari: ततो रूपगुणौदार्य संपन्ना प्राह केशवम् । उत्तरीयान् तमकृष्य स्मयन्ती जातहृच्छया ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: tato rūpaguṇaudārya saṃpannā prāha keśavam uttarīyān tamakṛṣya smayantī jātahṛcchayā

translation: Being thus gifted with excellent beauty and noble qualities, she thereupon felt the passion of love enkindled in her. And drawing him by the end of his upper garment, she requested him, beaming with a smile.

devanagari: एहि वीर गृहं यामो न त्वां त्यक्तुमिहोत्सहे । त्वयोन्मथितचित्तायाः प्रसीद पुरुषर्षभ ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: ehi vīra gṛhaṃ yāmo na tvāṃ tyaktumihotsahe tvayonmathitacittāyāḥ prasīda puruṣarṣabha
translation: “Come, Oh hero! Let us go home. I cannot bear (the idea of going away) leaving you here, now. Please be propitious unto a woman whose mind has been agitated by you. Oh prominent one among men.”

devanagari: एवं स्त्रिया याच्यमानः कृष्णो रामस्य पश्यतः । मुखं वीक्ष्यानुगानां च प्रहसन् तामुवाच ह ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ striyā yācyamānaḥ kṛṣṇo rāmasya paśyataḥ mukhaṃ vīkṣyānugānāṃ ca prahasan tāmuvāca ha
translation: When solicited thus by the woman, despite the presence of even Balarāma, Kṛṣṇa cast a glance at him and at the faces of cowherds, laughed loudly and spoke to her.

devanagari: एष्यामि ते गृहं सुभ्रु पुंसामाधिविकर्शनम् । साधितार्थोऽगृहाणां नः पान्थानां त्वं परायणम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣyāmi te gṛhaṃ subhru puṃsāmādhivikarśanam sādhitārtho
translation: “Oh lady with beautiful eye-brows! After accomplishing the object of my coming here (viz. killing of Kaṃsa) I shall certainly visit your house which relieves the pangs of hearts of men. You are the only resort of homeless wayfarers like us.[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Padaratnāvalī: God is not attached to anyone. But his devotees worship him and become his resort.
Bhāgavata Candrikā: You deserve to be enjoyed.

devanagari: विसृज्य माध्व्या वाण्या तां व्रजन् मार्गे वणिक्पथैः । नानोपायनताम्बूल स्रग्गन्धैः साग्रजोऽर्चितः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: visṛjya mādhvyā vāṇyā tāṃ vrajan mārge vaṇikpathaiḥ nānopāyanatāmbūla sraggandhaiḥ sāgrajo

translation: After taking leave of her in sweet words, as he proceeded through the commercial area of the town (lit. through the streets of the Vaiśya community), he, along with his elder brother and followers was worshipfully received with various presents, betel-leaves, garlands of pearls and precious Stones and perfumes by the guilds of merchants.

devanagari: तद्दर्शनस्मरक्षोभाद् आत्मानं नाविदन् स्त्रियः । विस्रस्तवासःकबर वलया लेख्यमूर्तयः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: taddarśanasmarakṣobhād ātmānaṃ nāvidan striyaḥ visrastavāsaḥkabara valayā lekhyamūrtayaḥ

translation: [2] The minds of the ladies of the merchant communities were so much agitated and overwhelmed with love that they forgot themselves. With loosened garments and braids of hair and slipping bracelets, they stood motionless as if painted in a picture.

devanagari: ततः पौरान् पृच्छमानो धनुषः स्थानमच्युतः । तस्मिन् प्रविष्टो ददृशे धनुरैन्द्रं इवाद्‍भुतम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ paurān pṛcchamāno dhanuṣaḥ sthānamacyutaḥ tasmin praviṣṭo dadṛśe dhanuraindraṃ ivād‍bhutam
translation: Thereupon, enquiring with the people about the venue of the Bow-(Sacrifice), the Immutable Lord (Kṛṣṇa) entered the sacrificial-hall and saw the wonderful bow, beautiful (and big) like the rainbow (as it was studded with precious stones).

devanagari: पुरुषैर्बहुभिर्गुप्तं अर्चितं परमर्द्धिमत् । वार्यमाणो नृभिः कृष्णः प्रसह्य धनुराददे ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: puruṣairbahubhirguptaṃ arcitaṃ paramarddhimat vāryamāṇo nṛbhiḥ kṛṣṇaḥ prasahya dhanurādade

translation: It was guarded by many men-at-arms, as it was worshipped and decorated with precious stones and valuables. Though he was prohibited by the attending guards, Kṛṣṇa forcibly caught hold of it.

devanagari: करेण वामेन सलीलमुद्धृतं । सज्यं च कृत्वा निमिषेण पश्यताम् । नृणां विकृष्य प्रबभञ्ज मध्यतो । यथेक्षुदण्डं मदकर्युरुक्रमः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: kareṇa vāmena salīlamuddhṛtaṃ sajyaṃ ca kṛtvā nimiṣeṇa paśyatām nṛṇāṃ vikṛṣya prababhañja madhyato yathekṣudaṇḍaṃ madakaryurukramaḥ

translation: Sportively picking it up with his left hand and fixing its string, Kṛṣṇa of formidable prowess, stretched it to the full and snapped it asunder in two in a second, as an intoxicated elephant would do to a sugarcane. And all men stood a-gazing (in astonishment).

devanagari: धनुषो भज्यमानस्य शब्दः खं रोदसी दिशः । पूरयामास यं श्रुत्वा कंसस्त्रासमुपागमत् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: dhanuṣo bhajyamānasya śabdaḥ khaṃ rodasī diśaḥ pūrayāmāsa yaṃ śrutvā kaṃsastrāsamupāgamat
translation: As the bow was being broken, the crashing sound filled the sky, the space between the earth and heaven and all the quarters. Hearing that sound, even Kaṃsa was seized with terror.

devanagari: तद् रक्षिणः सानुचरं कुपिता आततायिनः । गृहीतुकामा आवव्रुः गृह्यतां वध्यतामिति ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: tad rakṣiṇaḥ sānucaraṃ kupitā ātatāyinaḥ gṛhītukāmā āvavruḥ gṛhyatāṃ vadhyatāmiti

translation: The guards of the bow along with the attendants got enraged. Armed as they were, they were anxious to arrest him. Shouting “Let him be seized and bound down”, they encircled him.

devanagari: अथ तान् दुरभिप्रायान् विलोक्य बलकेशवौ । क्रुद्धौ धन्वन आदाय शकले तांश्च जघ्नतुः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: atha tān durabhiprāyān vilokya balakeśavau kruddhau dhanvana ādāya śakale tāṃśca jaghnatuḥ

translation: Perceiving those fellows of wicked intentions, Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa flared up in rage, and grasping the two pieces of the broken bow stick, soundly drubbed them all.

devanagari: बलं च कंसप्रहितं हत्वा शालामुखात्ततः । निष्क्रम्य चेरतुर्हृष्टौ निरीक्ष्य पुरसम्पदः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: balaṃ ca kaṃsaprahitaṃ hatvā śālāmukhāttataḥ niṣkramya ceraturhṛṣṭau nirīkṣya purasampadaḥ

translation: Having destroyed the battalion of soldiers despatched against them by Kaṃsa, they came out of the gate of the sacrificial hall. As they roamed along, they were delighted to see the affluence of the city.

devanagari: तयोस्तदद्‍भुतं वीर्यं निशाम्य पुरवासिनः । तेजः प्रागल्भ्यं रूपं च मेनिरे विबुधोत्तमौ ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: tayostadad‍bhutaṃ vīryaṃ niśāmya puravāsinaḥ tejaḥ prāgalbhyaṃ rūpaṃ ca menire vibudhottamau

translation: Witnessing their marvellous prowess, splendour, fearlessness and handsome personality, the citizens of Mathura regarded them as two foremost ones among gods.

devanagari: तयोर्विचरतोः स्वैरं आदित्योऽस्तमुपेयिवान् । कृष्णरामौ वृतौ गोपैः पुराच्छकटमीयतुः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: tayorvicaratoḥ svairaṃ ādityo kṛṣṇarāmau vṛtau gopaiḥ purācchakaṭamīyatuḥ

translation: While both of them were thus wandering at will, the sun set. Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma surrounded by cowherds, returned from the city to the camp of their carts.

devanagari: गोप्यो मुकुन्दविगमे विरहातुरा या । आशासताशिष ऋता मधुपुर्यभूवन् । संपश्यतां पुरुषभूषणगात्रलक्ष्मीं । हित्वेतरान् नु भजतश्चकमेऽयनं श्रीः...
english_devnagari: gopyo mukundavigame virahāturā yā āśāsatāśiṣa ṛtā madhupuryabhūvan saṃpaśyatāṃ puruṣabhūṣaṇagātralakṣmīṃ hitvetarān nu bhajataścakame

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: All the auspicious blessings anticipated (to happen to the citizens of Mathura) by the Gopīs who became afflicted with the pangs of (impending) separation from Kṛṣṇa at the time of his departure (from Vraja), materialised to be true at Mathura, in the case of those citizens who could gaze at the splendour of the elegant personality of Kṛṣṇa (The Supreme Person), the ornament of Manliness to whom Śrī, the goddess of beauty and affluence, resorted for shelter disregarding other gods who sought her.

devanagari: अवनिक्ताङ्घ्रियुगलौ भुक्त्वा क्षीरोपसेचनम् । ऊषतुस्तां सुखं रात्रिं ज्ञात्वा कंसचिकीर्षितम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: avaniktāṅghriyugalau bhuktvā kṣīropasecanam ūṣatustāṃ sukhaṃ rātriṃ jñātvā kaṃsacikīrṣitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ rā class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ rā class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ kṛ ], [accusative single from √ kṛ ]

translation: After washing their pair of feet, they partook of rice boiled in milk. With full knowledge what Kaṃsa intended to do the next day, they spent that night quite happily.

devanagari: कंसस्तु धनुषो भङ्गं रक्षिणां स्वबलस्य च । वधं निशम्य गोविन्द रामविक्रीडितं परम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: kaṃsastu dhanuṣo bhaṅgaṃ rakṣiṇāṃ svabalasya ca vadhaṃ niśamya govinda rāmavikrīḍitaṃ param
translation: Kaṃsa, however, was struck with panic when he heard the report of the breaking of the huge bow and the massacre of the guards of the bow and the contingent of the army (deployed against them), and the marvellous sportive acts of Śrī Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma. The wicked-minded fellow, being terrified, remained sleepless for a long time, and saw both in sleep and while awake, many evil omens portending death.

devanagari: दीर्घप्रजागरो भीतो दुर्निमित्तानि दुर्मतिः । बहून्यचष्टोभयथा मृत्योर्दौत्यकराणि च ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: dīrghaprajāgaro bhīto durnimittāni durmatiḥ bahūnyacaṣṭobhayathā mṛtyordautyakarāṇi ca

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: Kaṃsa, however, was struck with panic when he heard the report of the breaking of the huge bow and the massacre of the guards of the bow and the contingent of the army (deployed against them), and the marvellous sportive acts of Śrī Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma. The wicked-minded fellow, being terrified, remained sleepless for a long time, and saw both in sleep and while awake, many evil omens portending death.

devanagari: अदर्शनं स्वशिरसः प्रतिरूपे च सत्यपि । असत्यपि द्वितीये च द्वैरूप्यं ज्योतिषां तथा ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: adarśanaṃ svaśirasaḥ pratirūpe ca satyapi asatyapi dvitīye ca dvairūpyaṃ jyotiṣāṃ tathā
translation: Though his reflection in water or mirror was visible, he could not see his head in that reflection. Though no second luminary was in existence, he saw every planet (of lamp) in duplicate.

devanagari: छिद्रप्रतीतिश्छायायां प्राणघोषानुपश्रुतिः । स्वर्णप्रतीतिर्वृक्षेषु स्वपदानामदर्शनम् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: chidrapratītiśchāyāyāṃ prāṇaghoṣānupaśrutiḥ svarṇapratītirvṛkṣeṣu svapadānāmadarśanam
translation: He espied holes in his shadow. Nor could he hear the whizzing sound of vital breath when the ears were closed. He felt that the trees he saw were of gold. Nor his footprints (in dust or sand) as he walked, were visible to him.

devanagari: स्वप्ने प्रेतपरिष्वङ्गः खरयानं विषादनम् । यायान्नलदमाल्येकः तैलाभ्यक्तो दिगम्बरः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: svapne pretapariṣvaṅgaḥ kharayānaṃ viṣādanam yāyānnaladamālyekaḥ tailābhyakto digambaraḥ
translation: In dreams he saw himself clasped in embrace by dead persons (or goblins) and that he rode a donkey and gulped poison. He saw that he was wandering along with a garland of red flowers, and was smeared all over with oil and was naked.

devanagari: अन्यानि चेत्थं भूतानि स्वप्नजागरितानि च । पश्यन् मरणसन्त्रस्तो निद्रां लेभे न चिन्तया ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: anyāni cetthaṃ bhūtāni svapnajāgaritāni ca paśyan maraṇasantrasto nidrāṃ lebhe na cintayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: He perceived such and other similar portends foreboding death, both in his dreams and in the waking state. Being terribly afraid of death, he did not get any sleep at all through anxiety.

devanagari: व्युष्टायां निशि कौरव्य सूर्ये चाद्‍भ्यः समुत्थिते । कारयामास वै कंसो मल्लक्रीडामहोत्सवम् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: vyuṣṭāyāṃ niśi kauravya sūrye cād‍bhyaḥ samutthite kārayāmāsa vai kaṃso mallakrīḍāmahotsavam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [locative single from √ sūr class 4 verb], [locative single from √ sūr class 10 verb]

[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: When the night was over and the sun came up from the eastern waters, Karñsa caused (i.e. gave orders for) the grand celebration of athletic sports (such as wrestling of athletes etc.).

devanagari: आनर्चुः पुरुषा रङ्गं तूर्यभेर्यश्च जघ्निरे । मञ्चाश्चालङ्कृताः स्रग्भिः पताकाचैलतोरणैः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: ānarcuḥ puruṣā raṅgaṃ tūryabheryaśca jaghnire mañcāścālaṅkṛtāḥ sragbhiḥ patākācailatoraṇaiḥ
translation: Men worshipped the wrestling arena. There was a fanfare of trumpets and beating of drums. The galleries were decorated with garlands, flags, buntings and ornamental arches.

devanagari: तेषु पौरा जानपदा ब्रह्मक्षत्रपुरोगमाः । यथोपजोषं विविशू राजानश्च कृतासनाः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: teṣu paurā jānapadā brahmakṣatrapurogamāḥ yathopajoṣaṃ viviśū rājānaśca kṛtāsanāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[imperative active second plural], [injunctive middle third single]

translation: Citizens and country people headed by Brāhmaṇas and Kṣattriyas entered and were comfortably seated, each according to his position. Princes and royal personages also occupied their respective seats.

devanagari: कंसः परिवृतोऽमात्यै राजमञ्च उपाविशत् । मण्डलेश्वरमध्यस्थो हृदयेन विदूयता ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: kaṃsaḥ parivṛto maṇḍaleśvaramadhyastho hṛdayena vidūyatā
translation: Surrounded by his ministers and at the centre of his tributary princes sat Kaṃsa on the throne, though his heart was aching with anxiety.

devanagari: वाद्यमानेसु तूर्येषु मल्लतालोत्तरेषु च । मल्लाः स्वलङ्कृताः दृप्ताः सोपाध्यायाः समाविशन् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: vādyamānesu tūryeṣu mallatālottareṣu ca mallāḥ svalaṅkṛtāḥ dṛptāḥ sopādhyāyāḥ samāviśan

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ vad ], [vocative dual from √ vad ], [accusative dual from √ vad ], [locative single from √ vad ]
[nominative dual from √ vad ], [vocative single from √ vad ], [vocative dual from √ vad ], [accusative dual from √ vad ]

translation: While the flourish of trumpets was being (occasionally drowned by the stroking of arms by athletes[3], the well adorned proud athletic champions occupied their seats along- with their trainers.

devanagari: चाणूरो मुष्टिकः कूतः शलस्तोशल एव च । त आसेदुरुपस्थानं वल्गुवाद्यप्रहर्षिताः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: cāṇūro muṣṭikaḥ kūtaḥ śalastośala eva ca ta āsedurupasthānaṃ valguvādyapraharṣitāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kū class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kū class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kū class 6 verb], [nominative single from √ kū class 9 verb]

translation: Cāṇūra, Muṣṭika, Kūṭa, Śala and Tośala as well—all highly cheered up by the exciting music of the instruments presented themselves into the arena.

devanagari: नन्दगोपादयो गोपा भोजराजसमाहुताः । निवेदितोपायनास्ते एकस्मिन् मञ्च आविशन् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: nandagopādayo gopā bhojarājasamāhutāḥ niveditopāyanāste ekasmin mañca āviśan
translation:
Cowherd Nanda and other cowherds who were invited by the king of Bhojas and who had offered their presents to him, took their seats on a separate dais.
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे पूर्वार्धे मल्लरङोपवर्णनं नाम द्विचत्वारिंशोऽध्यायः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe pūrvārdhe mallaraṅopavarṇanaṃ nāma dvicatvāriṃśo
